titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to Collection of historical wire recordings
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Anne Rhodes

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday March 24, 2025 at 20:02

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Thecollection is arranged into series according to broadcast company.

did.unittitle: NBC Broadcast (?): Railroad Hour: Count Desilo (?); Countess Maritza

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lurene Tuttle (?), William Johnston, Enrique Carman (?), Gene Halloway, Blanche Thebom, Jerome Heins, Gordon McRae

did.unittitle: NBC Broadcast: Telephone Hour with Jerome Hines

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rodgers and Hammerstein: Oklahoma - Oh, What a Beautiful Mornin'. Mozart: Piano Sonata No. 11 in A Major "Rondo a la Turk" - arrangement for orchestra. Santa Lucia. Mozart: Don Giovanni. Victor Herbert: The Red Mill

did.unittitle: NBC Broadcast (?) Songs from the Firestone (?); Telephone Hour (?)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Johann Strauss II: Die Fledermaus - "Rosalinda"; Sandra Gould, Howard McNear, William Reeve. Jerome Kern: Sally - "Look for the Silver Lining"; Risë Stevens and the Firestone Chorus. "Sweet and Low"; performer announcement cut off. Donizetti: La favorite - "O mio Fernando"; Risë Stevens. Ernesto Lecuona: Ciboné(???); Risë Stevens. Paint Your Wagon - "I Still See Eliza"; Ezio Pinza. Beethoven: "In questa tomba oscura"; Ezio Pinza. Cavalli: ???; Ezio Pinza, Donald Voorhees, Bell Telephone Orchestra

did.unittitle: NBC Broadcast: NBC Symphony Orchestra, cond. Arturo Toscanini

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brahms: Variations on a Theme by Haydn; Brahms: Symphony No. 4 in E minor

did.unittitle: NBC Broadcast: NBC Symphony Orchestra, cond. Massimo Freccia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Berlioz: Roman Carnival Overture; Haydn: Symphony No. 101; Liszt: Les Preludes

did.unittitle: NBC Broadcast from the Berkshire Music Center at Tanglewood, Lennox, MA; Ben Grauer, announcer; Boston Symphony Orchestra; Serge Koussevitzky, cond.; second in a series of five Tanglewood concerts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mozart: Symphony No. 31 in D Major "Paris," k. 297/300a. Mozart: Symphony No. 39 in Eb Major, k. 543

did.unittitle: NBC Broadcast: NBC Symphony Orchestra, cond. Arturo Toscanini

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mozart: Overture to Magic Flute; Beethoven: Symphony No. 3 in E-flat

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Haydn: String Quartet in G Major, op. 76, no. 1; Haydn: String Quartet op. 33, no. 3 "The Bird"

did.unittitle: NBC Broadcast: NBC Symphony Orchestra, NBC String Quartet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First piece unknwon; Mozart: String Quartet No. 22 in B-flat, KV 589; Mozart: String Quartet No. 21 in D, "Minuetto," KV 575

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beethoven: String Quartet No. 10 in E-flat, op. 74; Haydn: String Quartet in F, op. 27, no. 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Schubert: String Quartet in E-flat, op. 125; Haydn: String Quartet no. 83 "Ultimate," op 103

did.unittitle: Hollywood Bowl Concert (3rd in Series of 8); NBC Radio: Symphonies Under the Stars; NBC Broadcast: NBC String Quartet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Weber: Overture to Der Freishütz; Hollywood Bowl Symphony Orchestra, (Alfred Wallenstein, cond.); Zoltan Kodaly: Hary Janos Suite; Hollywood Bowl Symphony Orchestra; Mendehlsohn: Violin Concerto; Hollywood Bowl Symphony Orchestra; Yehudi Menuhin (cond.?); Mozart: String Quartet in F Major k. 590; NBC String Quartet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Haydn: String Quartet No. 74 in G, op. 77 no. 1; Schumann (arr. Cooley): Bird as Prophet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Juan de Aragüés: String Quartet No. 1 in D minor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Verdi: Don Carlo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Richard Wagner: Lohengrin - Intro to Act III; unknown piece (Eleanor Steber); Giuseppe Verdi: La forza del destino - "Fatal Urn of My Destiny" (Arthur Butney); "La mento de Federico" (Charles Anthony); Ruggero Leoncavallo: Zaza - "Piccola zingara" (?) (Arthur Butney); Giacomo Puccini: Tosca - "Recondita armonia" (Charles Anthony); Puccini: La bohème - "Ah Mimi..." (Charles Anthony and Arthur Butney): ABC Symphony, cond. Fausto Cleva

did.unittitle: Met Broadcast: saturday matinee period (continued), cond. Fritz Stiedry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Richard Wagner: Parsifal (Jerome Hines, Hans Hope, Hans Horter, Paul Frank, Margaret Harshaw)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Georges Bizet: Carmen (Lucielle Browning, Risë Stevens)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Puccini: Gianni Schicchi

did.unittitle: Sermon and church service, possibly at Hamilton Presbyterian Church
Unknown soprano [probably Rosa Ponselle] informally singing with piano

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Italian songs; the first part of "D'amor sull'ali rosee" from Verdi's Il Trovatore; "Some Enchanted Evening" from Rogers and Hammerstein's South Pacific

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (informal social gathering with musical selections performed by Rosa Ponselle, later joined by Carmela Ponselle) Published on LP in limited release; possibly original wire recording. A selection of songs and duets, including Franz Schubert's "Ave Maria."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Barcarolle (Tales of Hoffman); Comin' Through the Rye

did.unittitle: Collector's Corner with Lloyd Garrison and Mabel Garrison

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The following pieces are announced, but not played on this recording: Dixie, Mabel Garrison and Victor Male Chorus; Offenbach: The Doll Song (from The Tales of Hoffman); Rimsky-Korsakov: Hymn to the Sun (from The Golden Cockerel; Norweigan Echo Song; Saints-Saens: Palisades, The Nightingale and the Rose; Vous dans le Marquis; Je Suis Tatania (from Mignon)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interview about making records

did.unittitle: Cook Sisters Talk; Collector's Corner with Lloyd Garrison: Great Moments in Song

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The following pieces are announced, but not played in the recording. Mozart: Bravo, signore padrone (from The Marriage of Figaro) sung by Ezio Pinza; Home Sweet Home", sung by Rosa Ponselle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gioachino Rossini: Inflammatus (from Stabat Mater); Eleanor Steber, Firestone Chorus: Amilcare Ponchielli: Voce di donna (from La gioconda); unknown; Florence Price: My Soul Has Been Anchored In The Lord (Donald Vorhees, Bell Telephone Orchestra); Joseph Knapp: Open The Gates of the Temple (Blanche Thebom, Donald Vorhess, Bell Telephone Orchestra and Chorus).

did.unittitle: Voice of the Firestone; American Railroads

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Great Waltz (Gordon MacRae, Lucille Norman, Norman Luboff); unknown piece and singer; Gustav Ferrari(?); unknown song; Alporezo; Theodore Paxson: Chanadore; Theadore Paxson: Sally Brown; Donald Vorhees, Bell Teleophone Orchestra and Chorus

did.unittitle: Railroad Hour: American Railroads. Announcer, Marvin Miller/Gordon MacRae (?); Voice of the News Post; Voice of Firestone.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sigmund Romberg: My Maryland; Gordon MacRae, Dorothy Kirsten, Norman Luboff, Carmen Ragan(?) (cond). Joseph Meyers: "California, Here I Come"; Hart Marlow (cond.) Firestone Orchestra and Chorus, Lois Hunt. "Someday"; Hart Marlow (cond.) Firestone Orchestra and Chorus, Lois Hunt. "Jewel Song" from Faust; Hart Marlow (cond.) Firestone Orchestra and Chorus, Lois Hunt. Battle Hymn of a Republic; Hart Marlow (cond.) Firestone Orchestra and Chorus, Lois Hunt. Entrance of the Little Fauns; Hart Marlow (cond.) Firestone Orchestra and Chorus, Lois Hunt. Dance of the Cavalristi; Hart Marlow (cond.) Firestone Orchestra and Chorus, Lois Hunt. Edward Boatner: "Oh What a Beautiful City";Donald Vorhees (cond), Bell Telephone Orchestra, Cumming.

did.unittitle: test (?): Rosa Ponselle (?), singers with organ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mozart: Violin Concerto No. 4 in D; Jascha Heifetz, Royal Philharmonic Orchestra (Thomas Beecham, cond.); Haydn: Symphony No. 102 in B-flat; Boston Symphony Orchestra (Serge Koussevitzky, cond.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mozart Symphony (?); Mozart: Piano Concerto No. 20 in D Minor; Rudolph (?) Philharmonic Orchestra (?)

did.unittitle: Organ recital by Daniel Pinkham; orchestral works

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Johann Caspar Ferdinand Fischer: Ariadne musica (performed by Daniel Pinkham). Maurice Ravel: Mother Goose Suite; Nicolai Rimsky-Korsokav: Scheherazade
